title: 17. Normal Mode Analysis ProDy ANM/GNM
desc: AlphaFold gives a static snapshot. Normal Mode Analysis reveals collective motions — the functional dynamics a protein needs to work. If truncation destroys the lowest-frequency modes, the protein may fold correctly but move wrong. We compared full STRC vs mini-STRC (700-1775) using Anisotropic Network Model (ANM, 15 A cutoff) and Gaussian Network Model (GNM, 10 A cutoff).
key_finding: Modes 1-20 of full STRC are >92% localized to the disordered N-terminal (residues 1-699). The first mode involving the structured core appears at mode 21. The N-terminal contributes only floppy, non-functional motions and is dynamically independent from the functional core.
overlap_desc: Do the same residues move the same way after truncation? Overlap >0.7 = excellent in the NMA literature.
overlap_cumulative: Cumulative subspace overlap (top 3 modes): 0.905 — outstanding. Functional dynamics are preserved.
mode3: Mode 3: Axial twisting with central hinge around residue 1113
bfactor: B-factor correlation: GNM log-transformed Pearson r = 0.57 (good), ANM Pearson r = 0.83. Mobility profile qualitatively preserved; absolute amplitudes differ (expected when removing 40% of the protein).
conclusion: Verdict: The N-terminal is not just structurally disordered — it is dynamically decoupled. It does not participate in the collective motions of the structured core. Removing it does not perturb the essential dynamics needed for stereocilia adhesion function. Subspace overlap 0.905 is strong evidence that mini-STRC retains full functional dynamics.
